text:
**Fueling the Ceasefire Window**

India is moving fast to exploit a two-week ceasefire, pressing Tehran to expedite oil and gas cargoes to rebuild depleted fuel stocks. With sixteen India-flagged ships stranded and a massive backlog of 800 vessels in the region, the race is on to secure unhindered transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

However, "full normalcy" is months away. Broken energy infrastructure, limited insurance availability, and the constant risk of ships getting stranded again mean recovery will be a slow grind. To keep domestic prices stable, New Delhi is now banking on a critical US waiver extension for Russian oil, providing a necessary buffer while the Gulf stabilizes.

INDIA ELECTRIFICATION THEME: STRUCTURAL MEGATREND

• Power demand surge driving multi-year capex cycle across T&D ecosystem

• Peak demand: 250 GW → 380–400 GW (next few years)  
• Rising middle-class consumption & AC penetration  
• EV adoption + manufacturing push  
• Data centre expansion

• Transmission & distribution infra lagging generation

• Order books: 2–3x revenue  
• Capacity utilization: 85–90%+  
• Lead times: 12–18 months  
• ROE: 20–25%+ | Earnings growth: 30–40%  

SECTOR STRUCTURE  
• Value chain: Transformers, cables, EPC, grid tech, smart meters  

RISKS  
• High valuations in select names (40–60x PE)  
• EPC risks: working capital, execution delays  
• High dispersion → stock selection critical  

INVESTMENT TAKEAWAY  
• Rare combo: strong demand + earnings visibility + rising returns  
• Multi-year opportunity (3–5 years) if execution sustains  

IMPACT  
• Positive – Early-stage infra cycle with strong long-term tailwinds

• Another year of high-quality compounding with strong AUM growth, profitability & industry tailwinds

• Revenue: ₹1,200–1,253 Cr (↑ 22–28% YoY)

AUM & MIX

CLIENT PROPOSITION  
• Focus on risk-adjusted returns & lower volatility

• Beta: 0.57 | Strong alpha generation  
• ₹1.85 lakh Cr assets under influence via audit-led model  

CONSISTENCY & QUALITY  
• 16-quarter PAT growth avg ~32% with low volatility  
• Long-term CAGR (FY19–26): Revenue 23%, PAT 31%, AUM 26%  

BALANCE SHEET & RETURNS  
• Net worth: ~₹1,012 Cr | Cash: ₹241 Cr  
• Strong dividend + bonus track record  
• Proposed 1:1 bonus (FY27)  

FY27 GUIDANCE  
• Revenue: ₹1,415 Cr  
• PAT: ₹460 Cr  
• AUM: ₹1.2 lakh Cr  

INDUSTRY TAILWIND  
• MF AUM to GDP: ~6.1% (low vs global peers)  
• Equity MF AUM growing strongly (₹8.9L Cr → ₹35.4L Cr)  
• Rising HNI population + financialization trend  

KEY TAKEAWAY  
• Scalable, capital-light model with strong execution & structural tailwinds  

IMPACT  
• Positive – High-quality compounder with long runway for growth

text:
India battery demand is exploding for EVs and grid storage (to handle solar/wind) 

Government PLI scheme (₹18,100 crore) aimed for 50 GWh cell capacity by 2026 but as of late 2025/early 2026, only 1.4–few GWh commissioned. Imports still meet 90–100% of cell needs.

These gigafactories are annual production (GWh per year), not total batteries sitting in the grid. 

To store massive solar power, India must build factories and install batteries over many years. Even if all three hit full speed by 2028–29, it will take time to catch up.

BMW India EV Share Reaches 25%

Q1 Sales Record
- 4,567 units delivered in Q1CY26 (+17% YoY), highest ever.
- EVs now ~25-26% of total sales mix, up from ~10% prior.

EV Volume Surge

- Traction from iX1, iX, i7, MINI EV models.

Segment Dominance
- Holds ~70% luxury EV market share.
- Entry-level EVs from ~₹51 lakh drive adoption.

Competitor View
- Mercedes: ~10% EV penetration.
- Audi: EV sales paused due to supply issues.

Support Factors
- 6 EV models + 6,000+ charging points nationwide.
- Luxury EV market at tipping point with rising launches.

Govt Plans PLI Scheme for Polysilicon

Scheme Objective
- MNRE & Finance Ministry discussing PLI for polysilicon production.
- Targets upstream solar supply chain self-reliance.

Strategic Importance
- Polysilicon key raw material for wafers, cells, modules.
- Reduces heavy dependence on Chinese imports.

Current Landscape
- India capacities: ~172 GW modules, ~65 GW cells.
- Strong module growth but upstream import reliance persists.

Value Chain Push
- PLI to cover ingot-to-module end-to-end.
- Supports renewable energy atmanirbharta goals.

Challenges Ahead
- High capex needs; global market dominated by incumbents.
- No ALMM norms yet for polysilicon.

Expected Impact
- Bridges manufacturing gap for complete solar ecosystem localisation.

Financial Strengthening
- Focus on deleveraging and balance sheet cleanup.

Cash Flow Improvement

Pharma Market Growth at 5-Quarter High in Q4FY26

Robust Quarterly Performance
- India pharma market grew ~10.5% YoY, strongest in 5 quarters.
- Overall Q4 growth: ~10.46% (Mar'26); monthly trend steady at 10-11%.

Growth Drivers
- Volume growth recovered to ~1.7% from near-flat levels.
- Price growth stable at ~5.4–5.5%.
- New launches contributed ~3.3% to expansion.

Therapy-Wise Strength
- Cardiac: ~14–15% growth.
- Anti-diabetic: ~15%+.
- Vaccines: ~22–30%.
- Neurology: ~11–12%.

Market Shift

Luxury Car Makers Now Watching Petrol Prices?
BMW Group India President recently said that higher fuel prices and the push for ethanol blending may speed up the shift to electric vehicles (EVs) in India.
My simple question is this:
Since when did luxury car companies start checking petrol prices so closely?
Luxury cars were always sold as a symbol of success – not something you buy after calculating running cost.
From my personal experience, this attitude already exists in luxury showrooms.
The moment you ask the sales executive about the car’s average (mileage), their interest drops instantly.
By default, they judge you – “Yeh average dekhne wala aadmi luxury car nahi kharidega.” They lose energy and treat you differently.
What do you think?

RBI Keeps Repo Rate at 5.25%

- RBI left the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% in the first FY27 policy.
- MPC unanimously maintained a neutral stance, indicating a data-dependent approach.
- No immediate signal of rate hikes or cuts.
- FY27 GDP growth is projected at around 6.9%.
- CPI inflation is estimated at around 4.6%, while core inflation is near 4.4%.
- The decision reflects global uncertainty, West Asia tensions, and rising crude-linked risks.
- RBI flagged possible pressure from supply chains, freight costs, and a strong US dollar.
- The economy may be entering a “Goldilocks phase” of balanced growth and inflation.
- This keeps room open for rate cuts in the short to medium term, but inflation remains the main anchor.
- Growth outlook has been slightly moderated, with Q1 and Q2 revised to around 6.8%.
- Banking system may benefit from capital support measures.
- Overall, RBI is taking a cautious stance to balance growth support with inflation control.

Announced to acquire 100% stake in Bosch Chassis Systems India, a group entity focused on automotive safety solutions including anti-lock braking systems (ABS), electronic stability control (ESC) and advanced braking technologies, for a total consideration of Rs.90.7bn

Transaction is largely cash funded, with a smaller equity component.

BREAKING: President Trump is considering a plan to "punish" some NATO countries by moving US troops out of countries which he deemed "unhelpful" to the Iran War, per WSJ.

Details include:

1. The proposal would involve moving US troops from "unhelpful" countries and into countries who were "more supportive" of the Iran War

2. The plan is early in conception and one of several the White House is discussing to punish NATO

3. The US currently has around 84,000 troops stationed across Europe

4. Countries that could benefit because they are viewed as supportive include Poland, Romania, Lithuania, and Greece, US officials said

US-NATO relations appear to be degrading.

BREAKING: Iran has told mediators it will be limiting the number of ships crossing the Strait of Hormuz to around 12 per day and impose tolls under the ceasefire, per WSJ.

This is a sharp reversal from last night's statements from President Trump claiming a "complete opening" of the Strait of Hormuz.

Today, just 4 ships have passed through the Strait of Hormuz, the fewest of any day in April so far.

Why HVDC is Critical for Renewables:

Long-Distance Transmission: Ideal for connecting remote renewable sources (like offshore wind or desert solar) to cities with minimal power loss over long distances or undersea.

Grid Stability & Intermittency: HVDC provides superior control over power flow, allowing operators to stabilize voltage and frequency fluctuations caused by intermittent renewable sources.

Connecting Offshore Wind: HVDC is the standard for bringing offshore wind energy to shore efficiently using submarine cables.

Integrating Different Grids: It can link asynchronous power grids (running at different frequencies), which helps in trading renewable energy across regions.

Lower Transmission Costs & Infrastructure: Requires fewer conductor cables than AC, lowering material costs and reducing environmental footprint for long-distance bulk power transfer.

The Indian HVDC transmission market is experiencing rapid growth, valued at USD 3.86 billion in 2025 and projected to reach USD 5.84–6.31 billion by 2030-2031, expanding at a CAGR of 8.55%–12.6%. Driven by 500 GW renewable energy targets and long-distance power transfer needs

**India's Search for Balance**

India’s 2025 search trends reveal a nation deep in "digital upgrade" but desperate for offline connection. AI adoption skyrocketed by 154%, yet searches for "occupational burnout" and "micro-retirement" highlight a growing work-life crisis.

Culturally, the shift is fascinating: spirituality has gone high-tech with Mahabharat AI, while social lives are moving toward "aesthetic outings" and the viral bhajan clubbing (up 26.9K%). From Gen Alpha’s parental controls to senior citizens seeking tech for aging, India is searching for ways to use bots to boost productivity while reclaiming physical joy through "fake weddings" and hiking trails.

**The End of the IPO Honeymoon**

The era of effortless "listing gains" is fading. After the post-2020 frenzy, the IPO market is cooling as one-year average returns plummeted to just 3.03% in 2025—a sharp reality check for retail investors. In 2024, only 4 companies were trading above their issue price a year later, compared to 52 in 2021.

With ₹1.75 trillion in the pipeline, the market is shifting from "liquidity-driven" to "fundamental-led." **INDIA NUCLEAR PROGRAM: KALPAKKAM FBR MILESTONE**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• India’s 500 MWe Fast Breeder Reactor (FBR) at Kalpakkam reaches critical milestone  

**WHAT IT MEANS**  
• Part of India’s 3-stage nuclear program (Uranium → Plutonium → Thorium)  
• FBRs can “breed” more fissile material than they consume  
• Key step toward long-term thorium-based energy strategy  

**REAL STRATEGIC VALUE**  
• Reduces dependence on imported uranium over time  
• Enables utilisation of India’s large thorium reserves  
• Strengthens long-term energy security  

**IMPORTANT REALITY CHECK**  
• Not “infinite energy” – still requires complex fuel cycle & infrastructure  
• Thorium cycle (U-233) is technologically challenging & not yet fully commercialised at scale  
• Multiple reactors & decades of deployment needed for meaningful share  

**GLOBAL CONTEXT**  
• Russia operates commercial FBRs; others remain limited/prototype stage  
• India among few nations pursuing full thorium roadmap  

**LONG-TERM OUTLOOK**  
• Potential to support a meaningful share of power mix over decades  
• Clean, baseload power with strategic independence benefits  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive (Long-term) – Major step in nuclear self-reliance, but gradual execution required

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
• Government allocates ~70% gas supply to key industrial sectors  

KEY DETAILS  
• Directive issued by Petroleum & Natural Gas Secretary Neeraj Mittal  
• Allocation based on pre-March 2026 bulk non-domestic LPG consumption  
• Overall sectoral cap set at ~0.2 TMT/day  

SECTORS IMPACTED  
• Tiles industry  
• Fertilizers sector  
• Other gas-intensive industrial users  

REAL STRATEGIC VALUE  
• Ensures priority gas supply to critical industrial sectors  
• Supports production stability amid supply constraints  
• Helps maintain cost competitiveness for gas-dependent industries  

IMPORTANT REALITY CHECK  
• Overall cap limits total availability despite higher allocation share  
• Allocation linked to historical consumption may restrict new entrants  
• Pricing dynamics and supply availability remain key variables  

LONG-TERM OUTLOOK  
• Encourages efficient gas utilization across industries  
• Potential shift toward alternative fuels if constraints persist  
• Policy-driven allocation likely to remain cyclical based on supply  

IMPACT  
• Positive – Improves visibility for tiles & fertilizers sectors, but capped supply limits upside

**China’s Export Resilience**

​The Middle East energy crisis is proving to be an unexpected windfall for China’s export juggernaut. While rivals struggle with skyrocketing fuel costs and supply chain shocks, China is leveraging its massive oil reserves and domestic energy supply to maintain production. This "supply-side resilience" is even reversing "China plus one" trends, as global clients prioritize stability over diversification.

​However, it’s not all smooth sailing. The conflict brings "imported inflation" that could squeeze already thin manufacturing margins, and global demand remains a wildcard. If the war persists, China is well-positioned to snatch more market share, further cementing its role as the world’s most reliable—and cushioned—factory floor.

**Earnings: The Market’s North Star**

Forty years of Sensex history reveal a humbling truth: arithmetic trumps stories. While sentiment and "re-rating" can spark short-term fireworks, long-term wealth is almost exclusively fueled by corporate earnings growth. Over most rolling 10-year periods, Sensex returns have mirrored earnings at a steady 10-12%.

The "myth of extraordinary returns" often stems from rare windows where earnings and valuation multiples expand simultaneously. Today, we are in an earnings-led phase where valuations are already demanding. The disciplined takeaway? Stop hunting for spectacular "re-rating" stories and bet on the quiet, durable growth of bottom-line profits.

Grid-Scale Transformer Lessons

India’s chaotic electricity demand is the ultimate "stress test" for AI. A new study shows that Temporal Fusion Transformers outperformed deeper models by effectively juggling historical data, calendar variables, and static metadata—achieving a 4.15% error rate despite unmetered agricultural loads and shifting monsoon patterns.

The lesson? Domain fit beats architectural hype. While sophisticated models failed, the transformer’s ability to "weigh" which inputs matter most proved that India’s grid complexity requires specialized reasoning, not just raw power. However, better forecasts are useless without the regulatory flexibility to act on them.

The West Asia war crisis is turning India into a serious contender for global data centre infrastructure. Google Cloud, AWS, and Microsoft Azure are all actively seeking larger co-location deals with Indian data centres right now. 

Demand has spiked 200-500 MW in just three to four weeks. Gulf-based clients are in a particular rush, signing contracts at $60 per kilowatt . These are numbers that would have seemed optimistic six months ago. 

The economics of setting up data centres in India is  compeliing.  Building a 100 MW AI facility in India costs $5-6 million versus $12-14 million in Singapore and the APAC region. Double the capacity with same amount of money if set up elsewhere . For a hyperscaler, that's essentially a second facility for free. 

The GPU capacity of leading players like Yotta has already been fully contracted by US-based providers, with deals ranging from $16 million for a year to $1.3 billion for four years.

**CITI ON RBL BANK: Strong Momentum Across Advances and Deposits | TP ₹390 | Buy**

**Key Highlights**  
- **Advances growth accelerated sharply** to **22% YoY / 11% QoQ**, up from **13.5% YoY** in 3Q.  
- **Retail advances** rose **18% YoY / 10% QoQ**, led by **secured retail** growth of **36% YoY / 17% QoQ** across **Prime LAP, Small LAP, gold loans and BBG**.  
- **Unsecured retail** stabilized, posting **2% QoQ growth** supported by **JLG disbursements** and stronger **cards-in-force**.  
- **Wholesale advances** climbed **27% YoY / 11% QoQ**; **commercial banking** was up **29% YoY / 9% QoQ**.  

**Deposit Momentum**  
- **Deposits accelerated** to **25% YoY / 16% QoQ**.  
- **Granular retail deposits** increased **16% YoY / 4% QoQ**.  
- **CASA surged 23% YoY / 26% QoQ**, lifting the **CASA ratio to 33.6%**; **LCR improved to 130%** and **LDR moderated to below 82%**.  

**Profitability & Outlook**

- **RBI approval granted to ENBD** is an additional positive.  
- Citi maintains **Buy** with a **target price of ₹390**.

**MACQUARIE ON BAJAJ FINANCE: Growth vs Profitability Trade-Off | TP ₹860 | Underperform**

**Key Highlights**  
- **FY26 AUM growth of 22% YoY** was in line with management guidance.  
- Excluding **Covid-hit FY21**, this marks the **lowest growth rate in 15 years**.  
- As Bajaj Finance scales, sustaining both **growth and ROA above 4.0%** may become increasingly difficult.  

**Funding Trends**  
- **Deposit growth has weakened**, with deposits falling from a peak of **20–22%** to around **16% of total borrowings**.  
- This signals a softer funding mix and limits operating flexibility.  

**Risks & Outlook**  
- Key upside risks include **faster-than-expected loan growth** and **further improvement in margins and ROA**.  
- Macquarie maintains **Underperform** with a **target price of ₹860**.

**MACQUARIE ON HDFC BANK: Strong Deposit Growth Eases Concerns | TP ₹1,200 | Outperform**

**Key Highlights**  
- **Deposit growth robust** at **14.4% YoY** and **8.6% QoQ**, outpacing industry trends.  
- Some flows may reflect **year-end adjustments**, but focus remains on **period-end data**, consistent with **bank and RBI reporting**.  
- As of **15 March**, system deposits grew **10.8% YoY**, placing **HDFC Bank 200–300 bps ahead** of the industry for FY26.  

**Operational Trends**  
- **Loan-to-deposit ratio (LDR)** now **below 95%**, continuing a **steady decline** over the past three years.  
- **Strong deposit momentum** should **reassure investors**, alleviating concerns over funding pressures.  

**Outlook**  
- Macquarie expects the bank’s **performance trajectory** to remain solid, maintaining an **Outperform rating** with a **target price of ₹1,200**.
Morgan Stanley upgrades Avenue Supermarts to 'Overweight ' rating, hikes target price to Rs 5,188.

**GOLDMAN SACHS ON TRENT: Near-Term Margin Pressures Persist | TP ₹4,080 | Neutral**

**Performance Overview**  
- **Revenue growth of ~18% YoY in 4QFY26**, up from ~16% in 3Q, supported by **~3–4% LFL growth** and **improving industry demand**.  
- **Store-led growth moderating** (~200bps lower QoQ) as **store additions normalize**, partly due to **Zudio overlap impact**.  

**Margins & Profitability**  
- **Operating leverage softens** with **costs rising alongside expansion**, resulting in **~9% YoY EBIT decline**.  
- **Raw material inflation** adds **downside risk to margins**, weighing on profitability.  

**Outlook**  
- **FY27–28 EPS estimates** remain **materially below consensus**.  
- Goldman Sachs **maintains Neutral**, revising **target price slightly down to ₹4,080** (from ₹4,110).

Avenue Supermarts In Focus - Morgan Stanley Upgrade

MS on Avenue Supermarts
Upgrade to Overweight from Equal-weight; Hike TP to Rs 5188 from Rs 4552

DMart reported strong Q4 revenue growth of 19%

Believe DMart is well placed to deliver better topline and could be a defensive play in the discretionary space

See accelerated network expansion and external tailwinds from higher inflation to aid DMart

**SMARTWORKS EXPANDS MULTI-CITY DEAL WITH GLOBAL CX LEADER**

**Strategic Expansion**  
- Deal now extended to **4 cities**, covering **5,000+ seats**.  
- **Mumbai addition** brings **1,150 new seats** in **Navi Mumbai**.  

**Financial Highlights**  
- **Total expected rental revenue** exceeds **₹155 crore**.  
- **Mumbai expansion** alone valued at **~₹51 crore**.  

**Industry Trends**  
- Rising **enterprise shift toward multi-city managed workspace partnerships**.  
- **Strong pipeline** with **₹4,700 crore committed rental revenue** as of **Q3 FY26**.

**GOLDMAN SACHS ON DABUR: Gradual Recovery Amid Uneven Growth | TP ₹490 | Neutral**

**Key Highlights**  
- **Revenue growth** in **mid-single digits YoY**, while **EBITDA growth outpaces sales**, indicating **margin expansion**.  
- **India business** showing **recovery**, led by **high single-digit growth**.  
- **Home & Personal Care (HPC)** segment delivering **mid-teens growth**, signifying **market share gains**.  

**Segment Trends**  
- **Healthcare and F&B segments** remain **soft**, impacted by **weather and muted demand**.  
- **International business** faces pressure in the **Middle East**, though **other regions remain resilient**.  

**Outlook**  
- Expect a **gradual recovery trajectory**, but **growth trends stay uneven**, supporting Goldman Sachs’ **Neutral stance** with a **target price of ₹490**.

**GOLDMAN SACHS ON MARICO: Strong Growth & Margin Tailwinds | TP ₹860 | Buy**

**Key Highlights**  
- **Revenue growth above 20% YoY**, showcasing **robust operating momentum**.  
- **India volumes** in **high single digits**, indicating **steady demand trends**.  
- **Value-added hair oils** growing **above 20%**, sustaining strong traction.  

**Margins & Segment Trends**  
- **Copra prices down ~35%**, driving **gross margin expansion**.  
- **Foods and premium personal care** businesses showing **encouraging recovery**.  
- **International business** maintains **high-teens growth** in constant currency terms.  

**Outlook**  
- **Volume recovery** and **margin tailwinds** underpin a **positive growth outlook**, prompting Goldman Sachs to **maintain Buy** with a **target price of ₹860**.

**MORGAN STANLEY ON TATA MOTORS PV: Demand Weakness Persists | TP ₹340 | Equal-Weight**

**Key Highlights**  
- **March Land Rover sales** down **16% YoY (8,100 units)**, though slightly improved vs February.  
- **Incentives surged 26% YoY**, signaling **soft demand conditions**.  
- **Q4 sales down 21% YoY (21,000 units)**, **flat QoQ** despite steeper discounts.  

**Regional Trends**  
- Including **Jaguar**, **US retail sales down 22% YoY (22,100 units)**.  
- **Incentives up 27% YoY**, reflecting **continued pressure on demand**.  

**Outlook**  
- **Wholesale volumes** likely remain **strong**, but **retail momentum** continues to **lag**, prompting Morgan Stanley to **maintain Equal-Weight** with a **target price of ₹340**.

**ELARA CAPITAL ON US PHARMA TARIFFS: Generics Exempt, Innovators at Risk**

**Key Highlights**  
- **US proposes 100% tariffs** on **patented drugs**, while **generics remain exempt** for now.  
- **Exemption for generics** is **positive for Indian pharma**, limiting near-term pressure.  
- **One-year review clause** adds **medium-term policy uncertainty**.  

**Sector Impact**  
- **Innovator companies** face potential risk, though **mitigating factors** exist.  
- **Orphan drugs** remain **exempt**, supporting niche portfolios.  

**Outlook**  
- **Near-term impact appears limited**, but **policy-driven risks** may resurface over the medium term.

**CITI ON BANK OF BARODA: Strong Growth Momentum | TP ₹360 | Buy**

**Key Highlights**  
- **Sequential advances growth >6%**, coming in **ahead of estimates**.  
- **Overseas advances** remain **resilient** despite ongoing **geopolitical tensions**.  
- **Q4 earnings** likely to benefit from **higher recoveries** on written-off accounts.  
- **Income tax refund** expected to **lower effective tax rate**, further supporting profitability.  

**Outlook**  
- Citi maintains a **Buy rating** with a **target price of ₹360**, citing strong operational momentum and improving earnings outlook.

**CITI ON PNB: Growth Above Guidance But Valuation Concerns | TP ₹115 | Sell**

**Key Highlights**

- **Gross advances** slightly **ahead of estimates**, indicating steady momentum.  
- **RAM segment (Retail, Agriculture, MSME)** expected to post **mid-teen growth**.  
- **Undisbursed sanction line of ₹1.02 lakh crore** to aid **corporate credit growth**.  
- Bank has **exceeded its loan growth guidance** of 11–12%, but **valuation remains a concern** prompting a **Sell rating**.

**CITI ON M&M FINANCE: Strong Growth Momentum Ahead | TP ₹425 | Buy**

**Key Highlights**

- **Disbursements and asset growth** gaining traction, supported by seasonal factors.  
- **Tractor and CV segments** driving overall growth performance.  
- **NIMs expected to moderate** as benefits from lower funding costs ease.  
- Forecast for **Q4:** **ROA at 2.5%**, **ROE at 15%**, indicating solid profitability.

**HSBC ON CABLES & WIRES: Strong Q4 Growth Despite Margin Pressure**

**Key Highlights**

- **Revenue growth robust in Q4**, led mainly by higher realisations.  
- **Profitability impacted** due to rising input costs and lower operating leverage.  
- **West Asia disruptions** weighing on **near-term earnings visibility**.  
- **Ratings:** Maintain **Buy** on **Polycab** and **RR Kabel**; **Hold** on **KEI Industries**.

**MORGAN STANLEY ON NMDC: Iron Ore Prices Surge**

**Key Highlights**

- **NMDC raises iron ore prices sharply**, backed by firm global trends and robust domestic steel demand.  
- **Strong restocking demand** continues to support healthy volume growth.  
- **China’s supply reforms** and rising shipments from **Guinea’s Simandou** may pose **downside risks** ahead.  
- **West Asia developments** remain a key **near-term monitor** for price stability.

**MORGAN STANLEY ON DMART: Upgraded to Overweight | TP ₹5,188**

**Key Highlights**

- **Q4 revenue growth at 19%**, well above recent trend.  
- **Faster store expansion** and **moderate inflation** seen as major tailwinds.  
- **Positioned as a defensive bet** within the discretionary sector.  
- **Revenue growth outlook** strong — expected to improve to **~19–20% in FY27–28**.

**MORGAN STANLEY ON BANKS & NBFCs: Growth Momentum Strengthens in 4QFY26**

**Key Highlights**

- Loan and deposit growth **accelerated YoY** across lenders, signaling improving momentum versus 3Q.  
- **4Q seasonality** aided stronger growth; system data tracking **ahead of expectations**.  
- **NBFCs** saw solid traction — **Bajaj Finance AUM up 22%+ YoY**, **L&T Finance retail loans up ~26% YoY**.  
- **HDFC Bank’s LDR** fell ~400bps QoQ, indicating robust deposit inflows.  
- **Asset quality** stable to improving across most lenders.  
- Focus now shifting to **FY27 outlook**, with attention on macro and geopolitical headwinds.

**GOLDMAN SACHS ON INDIA RETAIL: Inflation Risks Rising for FY27**

**Key Takeaways**

- Inflation risk expected to rise into 1HFY27, likely to weigh on discretionary demand.  
- Crude-linked raw materials (polyester, PU, EVA) trending higher — signaling margin pressure from 1QFY27.  
- **Metro Brands** remains **preferred pick** for its premium focus and strong ability to pass on higher costs.  
- **Page Industries** to benefit from ~2% price hike, full effect visible from 1QFY27.  
- **Trent** faces limited pricing power in value segment (**Zudio**), constraining margin recovery.  
- **Bata India** maintained at **Sell** amid weaker brand positioning and mounting cost pressures.

**BANK OF INDIA: MORGAN STANLEY MAINTAINS UNDERWEIGHT DESPITE STRONG GROWTH**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Morgan Stanley maintains Underweight with target price of ₹120, even as balance sheet growth and RAM segment remain strong  

**RATING & TARGET UPDATE**  
• Recommendation: Underweight  
• Target Price: ₹120  

**Q4 BUSINESS UPDATE**  
• Strong balance sheet growth momentum  
• Domestic deposit growth remains robust  
• Continued traction in key lending segments  

**KEY BUSINESS DRIVERS**  

• Deposit Growth:  
  – Healthy domestic deposit growth supporting funding base  
  – Strengthens liability franchise  

• RAM Segment Strength:  
  – Strong growth in RAM (Retail, Agriculture, MSME)  
  – Key driver for credit expansion and diversification  

• Balance Sheet Expansion:  
  – Overall growth remains healthy across segments  

**STRATEGIC TAKEAWAYS**  
• Strong operational momentum led by RAM and deposits  
• Diversified loan book improving stability  
• However, concerns likely around valuation, margins, or asset quality  

**IMPACT**  
• Neutral – Strong growth fundamentals offset by cautious stance from Morgan Stanley

**DMART: CLSA HIGH-CONVICTION OUTPERFORM WITH STRONG GROWTH MOMENTUM**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• CLSA reiterates high-conviction outperform with target price of ₹6583, driven by strong sales growth, store expansion, and improving pricing environment  

**RATING & TARGET UPDATE**  
• Recommendation: High-Conviction Outperform  
• Target Price: ₹6583  

**OPERATIONAL PERFORMANCE**  
• Revenue: ₹17,205 Cr — modest beat vs estimates  
• Sales Growth: ↑ 18.9% YoY, showing clear QoQ acceleration  

**KEY BUSINESS DRIVERS**  

• Strong Demand Momentum:  
  – Healthy traction across categories driving revenue growth  
  – Acceleration indicates improving consumption trend  

• Inflation Tailwind:  
  – ~3% inflation supporting pricing and realizations  
  – Aids topline growth without hurting demand  

• Aggressive Store Expansion:  
  – 58+ store additions  
  – Total store network reaches 500  
  – Strong footprint expansion driving future growth visibility  

**STRATEGIC TAKEAWAYS**  
• Scale advantage with continued store addition strategy  
• Inflation aiding realizations in value retail model  
• Consistent execution driving steady growth  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong growth visibility supported by store expansion, improving demand, and favorable pricing environment

**TVS MOTOR: GOLDMAN SACHS REITERATES BUY, RAISES TARGET PRICE**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Goldman Sachs maintains BUY with increased target price of ₹4100 (earlier ₹3830), citing strong pricing power and premium-led growth visibility  

**RATING & TARGET UPDATE**  
• Recommendation: Buy  
• Target Price: ₹4100  
• Earlier Target: ₹3830 (revised upward)  

**KEY INVESTMENT RATIONALE**  

• Strong Pricing Power:  
  – Ability to pass on input cost increases effectively  
  – Better margin protection vs peers  

• Superior Volume Visibility:  
  – Driven by upcoming premium product launches  
  – Strong positioning in high-growth segments  

• RM Inflation Cushion:  
  – Better placed to offset raw material cost pressures  
  – Limited impact from metal & crude inflation on volumes  

• Margin Tailwinds:  
  – ~35 bps margin expansion expected from PLI scheme benefits  

**STRATEGIC TAKEAWAYS**  
• Premiumisation strategy driving both growth and margins  
• Strong competitive positioning vs peers in current cycle  
• Policy support (PLI) adds incremental profitability  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong earnings visibility supported by pricing power, premium products, and structural margin tailwinds

Morgan Stanley On Financials

Q4 Initial Updates: Broadbased volume growth acceleration

Year-on-year loan growth accelerated for both banks and NBFCs

Year-on-year deposit growth accelerated for banks

Q4 is a seasonally strong quarter and sees back-ended growth, year-on-year growth accelerated notably

NBFCs that put out updates also saw higher year-on-year AUM growth vs. Q3

Bajaj Finance delivering >22% year-on-year, while L&T Finance saw 26% year-on-year in retail loans

These numbers were in general higher than estimates

HDFC Bank saw its loan-to-deposit ratio decline 400 bps quarter-on-quarter and 200 bps year-on-year as deposit growth outpaced loan growth

Asset quality updates put out by Mahindra Finance, L&T Finance and IDFC First Bank were also positive

Jefferies On Pharma

US Tariffs: Relief for Indian Generics; Sai Life most exposed CDMO

Major patented drug manufacturing hubs like Europe and Korea will face 15% tariffs

Companies with MFN deals and US investments will also be exempted

Among Indian firms, in a worst case scenario, estimate mid-single digit impact on FY27E EPS of Sun

Morgan Stanley On Iron Ore Prices Hike

NMDC increases iron ore prices sharply

See continued elevated seaborne iron ore prices

This is relatively negative for JSW Steel and Jindal Steel given their external iron ore requirements

China's steel supply reforms in play, coupled with higher shipments, should turn sentiment negative for seaborne iron prices

Key to watch is how the Middle-East situation evolves

Kotak Securities On Pharma

US tariff structure fundamentally pressurizes pharma companies

Need to relocate manufacturing to the US, negotiate MFN deals and form partnerships with those companies and countries that have secured favorable deals

Generics being excluded for now

SUN Pharma and CRDMOs are exposed to the risk

Assuming a nil tariff pass-through, expect a modest 1-2% EPS impact on SUN Pharma

The implications for innovator-focused CRDMOs are more nuanced

Nuvama On Sobha

Target Price ₹1631

Earlier Target ₹1885

Recommendation Buy

Healthy pre-sales; Launches surge in Q4FY26

Bengaluru can deliver volume growth

Threat of job losses due to Al and the ongoing war have led to a decline in housing sales in the country and increased risk perception of realty stocks

Goldman Sachs On Adani Ports

Target Price ₹1710

Earlier Target ₹1740

Recommendation Buy

Slight miss in guidance; coal volumes to pick-up in FY27

Re-start of the Tata Power plant increases visibility for volumes in FY27

HBSC On Marico

Target Price ₹940

Recommendation Buy

Sustains volume growth; foods growth improves

All-round strong performance

Remains preferred pick in consumer staples

Morgan Stanley On Dabur

Target Price ₹412

Recommendation Underweight

Q4: In-line Performance

Q4 consolidated revenue growth mid-single digits versus estimate +5%

EBITDA growth ahead of revenues

Management expects progressive domestic demand recovery, while remaining watchful around the geopolitical landscape
